stroke_01.title: Stroke and metabolic syndrome, NHANES 2011-2018
stroke_01.question: Is self-reported stroke history in US adults associated with metabolic syndrome components, pooling NHANES 2011-2018?

steps.description: Merge NHANES cycles G-J and apply adult inclusion criteria
Define metabolic syndrome components from lab variables
Apply survey design weights for cross-sectional analysis

causal_claims: Self-reported stroke history may be associated with metabolic syndrome components in cross-sectional NHANES data.
stroke_01.rationale: NHANES captures both stroke self-report (MCQ160F) and metabolic syndrome components (lipids, glucose, waist circumference) across cycles G-J, making this association feasible for cross-sectional analysis.
stroke_01.guardrail_notes: Outcome self-report may underestimate stroke prevalence; cross-sectional design precludes causal inference; metabolic syndrome requires clinical harmonization of lab thresholds.
